zh.lifeSummary: 當Anthony Pollard於1477年出生在英國英格蘭戴文韋 白 迪維頓時，他的父親Richard Pollard41歲，母親Margaret Cockworthy22歲。他於1502年與Petronella Chudleigh在英國英格蘭戴文霍武德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1545年享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: Pollard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: perhaps a nickname from an unrecorded Middle English pollard, a derivative of the Middle English verb poll ‘to clip’ or the noun poll ‘the head’. It may have denoted someone with a close‐cropped head or a big head.
from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Pollard, a pet form of Paul (Old French Pol). The surname has been established in both England and Ireland since the 13th century.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Anthony
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual English form of the old Roman family name Antonius, which is of uncertain (probably Etruscan) origin. The spelling with -th- (not normally reflected in the pronunciation) represents a learned but erroneous attempt to associate it with Greek anthos ‘flower’. In the post-classical period it was a common name, borne by various early saints, most notably a 3rd-century Egyptian hermit monk, who is regarded as the founder of Christian monasticism.

en.lifeSummary: When Anthony Pollard was born in 1477, in Way by Tiverton, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard Pollard, was 41 and his mother, Margaret Cockworthy, was 22. He married Petronella Chudleigh in 1502, in Horwood, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 6 daughters. He died in 1545, at the age of 68.

Il a épousé Petronella Chudleigh en 1502, à Horwood, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé en 1545, à l’âge de 68 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Anthony Pollard(이)가 1477년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 웨이 비 티버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Pollard(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Cockworthy(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1502년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 호우드에서 Petronella Chudleigh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1545년 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anthony Pollard родился в 1477 г. в местности веи би тивиртон, девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard Pollard было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret Cockworthy, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Petronella Chudleigh в 1502 г. в местности хаурвуд, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 6 дочерей. Он умер в 1545 г. в возрасте 68 лет.
